description:
You are acting as a mobile build engineer who needs to keep a lightweight audit of recent CI-build
    results in an SQLite database that can later be consumed by other parts of the pipeline.  
    Perform the whole task strictly from the Linux command line, using only standard utilities that are
    already present in a minimal Linux environment (e.g. bash, sqlite3, cat, echo, mkdir, etc.).
    No GUI tools or external libraries should be used.

    1. Directory layout
       a. Create the directory /home/user/mobile_pipelines/  
       b. Inside it, create an SQLite database file named builds.db  
       c. Create a sub-directory /home/user/mobile_pipelines/report/ that will host a plain-text
          summary report.

    2. Database schema – ALL names are case-sensitive
       a. Table   : builds  
          Columns :  
            id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
            commit_hash   TEXT    NOT NULL  
            status        TEXT    NOT NULL  (allowed values: SUCCESS or FAILED)  
            duration_sec  INTEGER NOT NULL  (build duration in whole seconds)  
            timestamp     TEXT    NOT NULL  (ISO-8601 string, UTC, e.g. 2023-09-01T12:01:30Z)

       b. Table   : artifacts  
          Columns :  
            id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
            build_id      INTEGER NOT NULL  (foreign key → builds.id)  
            artifact_name TEXT    NOT NULL  
            size_kb       INTEGER NOT NULL  (size in KiB)

    3. Data that must exist after you finish
       a. builds:  
          1 | a1b2c3d4 | SUCCESS | 320 | 2023-09-01T12:01:30Z  
          2 | e5f6g7h8 | FAILED  | 150 | 2023-09-02T14:22:10Z  
          3 | i9j0k1l2 | SUCCESS | 287 | 2023-09-03T09:47:55Z  

       b. artifacts:  
          1 | 1 | app-release.apk | 20480  
          2 | 1 | symbols.zip     | 5120  
          3 | 2 | crash-log.txt   | 12  
          4 | 3 | app-release.apk | 20512  

    4. Required query & report  
       Using the sqlite3 CLI, run the following conceptual query (do NOT hard-code its output; build it
       at runtime so an automated grader can decide if the query was really executed):

            SELECT commit_hash,
                   status,
                   COUNT(artifacts.id) AS artifact_count
            FROM   builds
            LEFT JOIN artifacts ON builds.id = artifacts.build_id
            GROUP  BY builds.id
            ORDER  BY builds.id;

       Redirect the formatted result into the text file  
       /home/user/mobile_pipelines/report/build_summary.log  
       so that the file content matches EXACTLY the seven lines shown below (no extra spaces at line
       ends, no additional blank lines):

            Build Summary Report

            Commit Hash | Status   | Artifact Count
            ----------------------------------------
            a1b2c3d4     | SUCCESS  | 2
            e5f6g7h8     | FAILED   | 1
            i9j0k1l2     | SUCCESS  | 1

       Notes on the expected formatting:
         • “Build Summary Report” is the first line.
         • The second line is completely empty.
         • Column header line uses exactly the text and spacing above.
         • A separator line of 40 dashes (“-”) follows.
         • Each data row shows: commit_hash (left-aligned, 9 chars wide), two spaces, the literal “|”,
           one space, status (left-aligned, 7 chars wide), two spaces, “|”, one space, the integer
           artifact_count.
         • UNIX line endings (\n).

    5. Verification hint (for you):  
       The grading script will
         • check that /home/user/mobile_pipelines/builds.db exists and is a valid SQLite database;  
         • verify that the two tables exist with the exact schema described;  
         • confirm that the six rows above are present;  
         • run the GROUP BY query itself and compare its result set to the contents of
           build_summary.log, byte-for-byte.

    Remember:
      • Do NOT include any literal SQL or shell commands in your answer here; you will supply them only
        through the terminal during the interactive session.
      • Ensure file/directory permissions allow the current non-root user (uid=1000, username “user”)
        to read/write them (default mkdir/touch behaviour is fine).
truth:
<!-- Paths -->
    /home/user/mobile_pipelines/builds.db must exist.
    /home/user/mobile_pipelines/report/build_summary.log must exist.

    <!-- Schema definitions -->
    builds table schema:
        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
        commit_hash TEXT NOT NULL
        status TEXT NOT NULL
        duration_sec INTEGER NOT NULL
        timestamp TEXT NOT NULL
    artifacts table schema:
        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
        build_id INTEGER NOT NULL
        artifact_name TEXT NOT NULL
        size_kb INTEGER NOT NULL

    <!-- Data that must be present -->
    builds rows:
      (1, 'a1b2c3d4', 'SUCCESS', 320, '2023-09-01T12:01:30Z')
      (2, 'e5f6g7h8', 'FAILED', 150, '2023-09-02T14:22:10Z')
      (3, 'i9j0k1l2', 'SUCCESS', 287, '2023-09-03T09:47:55Z')
    artifacts rows:
      (1, 1, 'app-release.apk', 20480)
      (2, 1, 'symbols.zip',     5120)
      (3, 2, 'crash-log.txt',   12)
      (4, 3, 'app-release.apk', 20512)

    <!-- Expected file content -->
    build_summary.log exact ASCII content (including newlines):
Build Summary Report

Commit Hash | Status   | Artifact Count
----------------------------------------
a1b2c3d4     | SUCCESS  | 2
e5f6g7h8     | FAILED   | 1
i9j0k1l2     | SUCCESS  | 1

    <!-- End -->
